description: Show what the engine is building — what's next, what needs your review, and known issues — at a glance on a GitHub Projects board. One-time setup creates a board on your GitHub account and asks for a permission that covers all your GitHub Projects (optional and revocable), plus an optional one-time click in GitHub to turn on auto-add. A board is never required: the engine works the same from your issues and pull requests, and you can delete the board later without losing anything.

Before a change you've asked for gets built, a fresh set of reviewers checks the plan first — whether it solves the right problem, whether the design is sound, whether it can actually be built and run, and whether it could be unsafe or break a rule it must follow. They only advise; you decide, and you merge.

After a change you've asked for is built — before it's submitted — a fresh set of reviewers checks the finished work: whether it does what was asked, whether it's pleasant to use, whether it's soundly built, and whether it's safe to release. They only advise; you decide, and you merge.

A standing rule for your project's database migrations — the scripts that change your database's shape or move its data. Before a change that could lose data or can't be undone, the engine is set to stop and bring you in — pausing to ask when you're working with it, and holding the work for you to see when it's running unattended — in plain language and with a safer option, instead of pressing ahead on its own. It's a habit the engine follows, backed by the pull request you review and approve, not an automatic scanner — and it never runs a migration for you. Optional, and removable anytime.
